items.questionBody: Can Ingebrigtsen return to top form? In 2025 his only races outdoors were the 1500m and 5000m at the World Championships in Tokyo where he finished eighth in the prelim and tenth in the final, respectively.
items.headlineRest: on the mend from a debilitating achilles injury.
items.name: Jakob Ingebrigtsen
items.reviewBody: Ingebrigtsen has yet to race in 2026. The Norwegian may return from racing in mid-July following surgery to remove damaged tissue and scarring from the sheath surrounding his left Achilles tendon in February of this year.

items.questionBody: Is this the year Hocker runs a sub-3:45? The talent is there. The question is whether he can find a race with the right pacemakers.
items.headlineRest: who has the kick to beat anyone in the world on the right day.
items.name: Cole Hocker
items.reviewBody: At just 23, Hocker is already running times that would have seemed impossible a generation ago for an American miler. His Olympic gold proved it wasn't a fluke.

items.questionBody: Can he find the gear to challenge Ingebrigtsen head-to-head? Their encounters so far have gone to the Norwegian, but the gap is narrowing.
items.headlineRest: who keeps showing up at the finish line when it matters most.
items.name: Isaac Nader

results.meet: Meeting International Mohammed VI d'Athletisme de Rabat
Campeonatos Nacionais de Clubes 1ª e 2ª Divisão
Campeonatos Nacionais de Clubes 1ª e 2ª Divisão

items.reviewBody: Kejelcha has been a model of consistency in 2026. While he hasn't grabbed the headlines, his results speak for themselves — four top-3 finishes in Diamond League events and a season best of 3:45.12.

items.questionBody: Can Kerr position himself for another world title? His head-to-head with Hocker and Ingebrigtsen will be the defining series of the season.
items.headlineRest: who turns middle distance into a strength competition and usually wins.
items.name: Josh Kerr
items.reviewBody: Kerr continues to be one of the most physically imposing presences in the 1500m field. His ability to surge mid-race and break the competition is unmatched.

items.questionBody: Is this a true comeback season or a false dawn? The Oslo Diamond League will be the real test.
items.headlineRest: who knows exactly where he needs to be at the bell and how to finish.
items.name: Timothy Cheruiyot

results.meet: Athletics Kenya Prison Services Track and Field Championships
Athletics Kenya Prison Services Track and Field Championships
Athletics Kenya Prison Services Track and Field Championships
Athletics Kenya Prison Services Track and Field Championships

items.reviewBody: Cheruiyot has shown signs of returning to his dominant best after a couple of injury-interrupted campaigns. His Shanghai win was a reminder that when he's right, he can run with anyone.

items.headlineRest: who is now all grown up and trying to prove his 2024 campaign was no fluke.
items.name: Hobbs Kessler

items.headlineRest: who made the switch to 1500m and won USAs.
items.name: Jonah Koech
